text:
Once upon a sun-kissed afternoon, in the quaint little town of Meadowvale, there lived a fox named Fred. Fred was no ordinary fox. He had learned to read books about far-off lands. Every weekend, he'd sneak into a nearby library tucked away behind lavender bushes.

One particular autumn evening, Fred found himself cozy beside a huge old walnut tree, devouring a book with a plot so intriguing it had him dancing through the forest late into the night. The book was about wizards crafting potions.

After finishing the last page, he imagined himself brewing up a potion that would allow him to talk like a human. For weeks, Fred practiced his human-like speech, humming fairy tales instead of singing.

Then one sunny winter afternoon, while nibbling on a carrot, Fred accidentally spilled a potion of his imagination onto the ground. To his astonishment, a small talking bird fluttered down.

"Hello," it chirped. "Who are you?

"Benny," Fred replied. "
